expectations.text: Produces both deliverables — a markdown behavior spec (.md) and a Cucumber .feature file
expectations.evidence: Output dir contains behavior-spec-teammate-invitations.md plus send-invitations.feature, accept-invitation.feature, manage-pending-invitations.feature. (The prompt explicitly asked for both doc and .feature files.)

expectations.text: The .feature file is well-formed Gherkin (Feature declaration, Scenario blocks, Given/When/Then steps)
expectations.evidence: Three standard .feature files with 32 Scenario blocks total covering sending, accepting, and managing invitations.

expectations.text: Happy-path scenarios are tagged @happy-path and match the invite flow described in the prompt
expectations.evidence: 0 @happy-path tags. The happy path is a numbered prose narrative (Section 6); .feature scenarios are untagged, so happy paths cannot be distinguished from deviations.

expectations.text: The spec includes a coverage checklist enumerating all 14 deviation taxonomy categories
expectations.evidence: No 14-category taxonomy checklist. Section 8 is an ad hoc 13-row 'Error & Edge Cases' table.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios span at least 8 distinct deviation categories
expectations.evidence: Edge cases + behavior rules touch ~8 categories (invalid input, state conflict, duplicate/retry, out-of-order, adversarial token, time/expiry, rate/seat limit, external failure).

expectations.text: Deviation scenario Then-steps specify concrete graceful product behavior, not merely that an error occurred
expectations.evidence: Section 8 edge-case table and Section 7 behavior rules give concrete graceful resolutions (e.g. malformed email flagged inline, valid addresses still send).

expectations.text: Genuine product decisions are tagged @assumption and consolidated into a Flagged Assumptions section
expectations.evidence: 0 @assumption tags. Section 5 lists some assumptions in prose but they are not tagged on scenarios, not consolidated as a review table, and not tied to the decisions they affect.

expectations.text: The .md spec includes the narrative sections Problem & Intent, Actors, Glossary, Preconditions, and Scope
expectations.evidence: Has Overview, Scope, Roles & Actors, Key Concepts & Definitions (glossary), and Preconditions & Assumptions — all five narrative sections present.

expectations.text: The happy-path scenarios include both the new-user (account creation) and existing-user (sign-in) acceptance sub-paths
expectations.evidence: accept-invitation.feature covers new user (account creation) and existing user (logged out and logged in); both sub-paths present.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios cover inviting an existing member, a seat or rate limit, an expired invitation, and a malformed email address
expectations.evidence: Edge-case table and feature files cover already-a-member, seat-limit gate, expired invitation, and malformed email.
